content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 22, 1990


Robert Wagner
P.O. Box 425
South Pasadena, CA  91031

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-90-078

Dear Mr. Wagner:

     This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice concerning your duties and responsibilities under
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We only provide advice
related to the provisions of the Act. Please consult the
Secretary of State and local ordinances which may impose ad-
ditional requirements on your campaign for city council.

QUESTION

     Is the name "Committee to Reelect Bob Wagner to City Council"
in compliance with the requirements of the Act?

CONCLUSION

     The name "Committee to Reelect Bob Wagner to City Council" is
in compliance with the requirements of the Act.

FACTS

     You served as a member of the city council of the City of
Pasadena from 1984 through 1988. You have decided to campaign for
the same office in 1990. You are seeking our advice to determine
whether use of the name "Committee to Reelect Bob Wagner to City
Council" would be in compliance with the provisions of the Act.

     During our telephone conversation of January 17, 1990, I
advised you that the name complies with the requirements of the
Act. Subsequently, I also mailed to you on the same date a copy

___________________________

¹ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

File No. A-90-078
Page 2

of the Act and copies of our Information Manual on Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act and Interim
Information Manual on Proposition 68 and Proposition 73.

ANALYSIS

The Act defines a committee as any person or combination of
persons who directly or indirectly receives contributions totaling
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year. (Sec-
tion 82013(a).) Committees are required to file a statement of
organization with the Secretary of State. (Section 84101(a).)

Additionally, a committee which is controlled directly or
indirectly by a candidate or which acts jointly with a candidate
in connection with the making of expenditures is a controlled com-
mittee. A candidate controls a committee if he, his agent, or any
other committee he controls has a significant influence on the
actions or decisions of the committee. (Section 82016.)

The Commission has interpreted the definition of "controlled
committee" very broadly to include any significant participation
in the actions of a committee by a candidate, his or her agent, or
representatives of any other committee he or she controls.
(Forcier Advice Letter, No. I-89-058, copy enclosed.)

The contents of the statement of organization of a controlled
committee must comply with specific requirements of the Act. Sec-
tion 84106, added to the Act by Proposition 68, has been
determined to be not in conflict with Proposition 73 and severable
from the other provisions of Proposition 68. (In re Bell (1988)
11 FPPC Ops 1, copy enclosed.) It requires that the name of a
committee include or be accompanied by the name of any individual,
entity or other person controlling the committee. (See also
Bagatelos Advice Letter, No. I-88-475, copy enclosed.)

The name "Committee to Reelect Bob Wagner to City Council"
includes your name as required by Section 84106. Accordingly, it
is in compliance with the requirements of the Act. The fact that
you are not currently a member of the city council has no bearing
on our conclusion. The Act does not regulate that aspect of your
committee's name.

Should you have any further questions regarding this matter
do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

ED:BMB:plh
Enclosures
